data.title: Financial Products Group Secures 16.5 Billion Yen Credit Line
data.description:
Financial Products Group secured a new 16.5 billion yen committed credit line agreement with MUFG Bank, effective March 25, according to its Wednesday bourse filing.

The facility, valid until March 30, 2026, will support funding for FPG's Leasing Fund Business, Domestic Real Estate Fund Business, and International Real Estate Fund Business.

This agreement replaces the existing credit line secured last March.

data.title: Commerzbank drops after CEO comments on higher risk results
data.description:
** Commerzbank's shares drop around 6% after CEO Bettina Orlopp said 2025 will be a year with higher risk results

** "Higher risk implies more bankruptcies in Germany," a local trader says

** If losses hold, shares will see their worst day since September 2023

** European banking index (.SX7P) was down 2.4% at 0949 GMT, after the U.S. Federal reserve left rates unchanged citing high uncertainty

data.title: Century Joint Developments accepts bids for bond issue, bankers say
data.description:
India's Century Joint Developments has accepted bids worth 7.50 billion rupees ($86.9 million) for bonds maturing in three years and eight months, two merchant bankers said on Thursday.

The construction company will pay a coupon of 10.00%, payable on a quarterly basis, and had invited bids from bankers and investors earlier in the day.

The company did not reply to a Reuters email seeking comment.

Here is the list of deals reported so far on March 20:

data.title: N. Macedonia orders Cranfield Foundry to return state aid
data.description:
SKOPJE (N. Macedonia), March 20 (SeeNews) - North Macedonia's government said it had ordered local iron castings producer Cranfield Foundry to repay 131.8 million denars ($2.3 million/2.1 million euro) of state aid and penalties.

Cranfield Foundry was ordered to repay the amount within 60 days, starting with a 100,000 euro payment and a bank guarantee by February 15, followed by 30 monthly installments from July 1, a government decision posted on its website in January showed.

Cranfield Foundry failed to invest 20 million euro as required by the state aid agreement signed in 2015, despite government extensions, news agency MMS reported last week.

The company initially planned to invest about 15 million euro in the construction of its plant in North Macedonia's Probishtip, but later increased the planned investment to 20 million euro to fund its expansion, according to earlier government statements. Cranfield Foundry's factory in Probishtip was open in 2019.

(1 euro = 61.34 denars)

Such disclosure should state clearly that it corrects details disclosed previously, identify the disclosure or disclosures being corrected, and provide sufficient detail for the reader to understand the nature of the corrections. In the case of any doubt, the Panel should be consulted.

For full details of disclosure requirements, see Rule 8 of the Rules. If in doubt, consult the Panel.

References in these notes to “the Rules” are to the Irish Takeover Panel Act, 1997, Takeover Rules, 2022.

data.title: Nexi rises after media report of offer for DBS
data.description:
** Shares in Nexi rise as much as 5.8% after a media report that US investment fund TPG has offered the Italian payments group around 850 million euros ($921 million)for its digital banking solutions division (DBS)

** Nexi declined to comment on the report

** Italian daily Corriere della Sera reported that the unit, with revenues of 390 million euros in 2024, includes the strategic Register of Italian Companies

** "We believe that the sale of the DBS division would represent a positive element for Nexi, consistent with the strategy of divestment of non-core assets with lower growth prospects," Equita says

** At 0928 GMT Nexi's shares were up 1.8%; up to Wednesday session's close they were down 2.2%​ YTD

($1 = 0.9225 euros)

data.title: JPMorgan Adjusts Price Target on Chipotle Mexican Grill to $58 From $64, Maintains Neutral Rating
data.description: Chipotle Mexican Grill has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $66.47, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Indian Hume Pipe shares jump 14% after land sale proposal
data.description:
** Shares of water projects construction firm Indian Hume Pipe rise as much as 14.1% to 376 rupees

** IHME board on Wednesday authorised its management on proposal to sell co's freehold land at Bengaluru, Karnataka measuring about 40,875 sq. mtrs

** Co didn't disclose the expected financial gain from the land deal

** Stock is witnessing its most active trading session since September 2 with more than 1 mln shares changing hands, 10.3x of 30-day avg

** Stock last up 5.4%, cutting YTD loss to 3.7%

data.title: Sisram Medical Posts Lower 2024 Profit
data.description:
Sisram Medical reported a profit attributable to the owners of $25.1 million for 2024, down from $31.5 million a year earlier.

Earnings per share were $0.0536, a decline from $0.0673 in the same period last year, according to a Wednesday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Revenue dropped 2.8% to $349.1 million from $359.3 million the previous year.

The board resolved to declare a final dividend of HK$0.126 per share.

data.title: Jefferies Adjusts Tencent Holdings' Price Target to HK$646 From HK$550, Keeps at Buy
data.description: Tencent Holdings has an average rating of buy and mean price target of HK$564.06, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Jiangxi Rimag to Swing to Loss in 2024
data.description:
Jiangxi Rimag Group expects to incur a net loss of between 55 million yuan and 60 million yuan in 2024, swinging from the year-ago profit of 36.6 million yuan, according to a Hong Kong Stock Exchange filing on Tuesday.

The medical imaging solution services company attributed the forecasted loss to the macroeconomic environment and a slowdown in demand, as well as the incurrence of certain expenses.

The actual financial results are set to be published on or before March 31, the filing said.

The company's shares closed less than 4% higher.

data.title: Iran says it will consider 'opportunities' as well as threats in Trump letter
data.description:
Iran will consider the "opportunities" as well as the threats in a letter from U.S. President Donald Trump that urged it to reach a new nuclear deal, and will soon respond, Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi said on Thursday.

Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei rejected Trump's letter last week as deceptive, saying Trump's excessive demands would "tighten the knot of sanctions and increase pressure on Iran".

But Araqchi said Tehran was still evaluating the letter and weighing its reply.

"Trump’s letter was more a threat, but it claims to have opportunities. We paid attention to all points held in the letter and will consider both threat and opportunity in our response,” Araqchi said.

"There is an opportunity behind every menace."

On Wednesday, Axios reported that Trump's letter gave Iran a two-month deadline to reach a nuclear deal or face stricter sanctions under the U.S. President's renewed "maximum pressure" campaign.

Araqchi said Tehran would respond to Trump's letter in the coming days via appropriate channels, rejecting any direct negotiations as long as Washington levels "pressure, threats and sanctions".

In his first term, Trump withdrew the U.S. from a 2015 deal between Iran and major powers that had placed strict limits on Tehran's nuclear activities in exchange for sanctions relief.

After Trump pulled out in 2018 and re-imposed sanctions, Iran breached and far surpassed those limits in the development of its nuclear programme.

Western powers accuse Iran of seeking nuclear weapons by enriching uranium up to 60% purity, above what they say is justifiable for a civilian programme.

Tehran says the development of its nuclear programme is for peaceful purposes and that it respects its commitments under international law.

data.title: European Smaller Companies Trust Swings to Fiscal H1 Loss
data.description:
The European Smaller Companies Trust said Thursday it swung to a loss in the first half of fiscal 2025, while investment income declined year over year.

Loss for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 59.9 million pounds sterling, compared with a profit of 32.9 million pounds earlier. Loss per ordinary share moved to 0.1516 pound from a return per ordinary share of 0.0821 pound.

The British investment trust's investment income was 5.5 million pounds, compared with 6.4 million pounds a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board declared an interim dividend of 0.0145 pound per share, payable May 2 to shareholders on record April 4. It paid the same amount per share a year ago.

Spain's Treasury announced the following results at Thursday's bond auction (BANCN) where it sold 6.48 billion euros ($7.03 billion) of bonds.

data.title: Tokmanni Group Corporation: Managers' Transactions - Tapio Arimo
data.description:
Tokmanni Group Oyj

Tokmanni Group Corporation        Managers' Transactions        20 March 2025 at 11:50 am

Person subject to the notification requirement

Name: Tapio Arimo

Position: Chief Financial Officer

 Issuer: Tokmanni Group Oyj

LEI: 743700VMG6KWF0FW1560

Further Information, please contact

Maarit Mikkonen, Head of IR and Communications, tel. +358 40 562 2282, maarit.mikkonen@tokmanni.fi

Tokmanni Group in brief

Tokmanni Group Corporation is one of the leading variety discount retailers in the Nordics. More than 6,000 employees in Finland, Sweden and Denmark make customers' everyday life and special occasions easier by offering a versatile and up-to-date assortment of nordic and international brand-name products and other high-quality products at prices that are always affordable. With more than 370 Tokmanni, Dollarstore, Big Dollar, Click Shoes and Shoe House stores and online stores, the Group is always close to its customers. In 2024, the Group's revenue was EUR 1 675 million and comparable EBIT amounted to EUR 100 million. The Tokmanni Group Corporation's shares are listed on Nasdaq Helsinki.

Distribution

Nasdaq Helsinki

data.title: Hutchmed's Profit Falls in 2024
data.description:
HUTCHMED (China) reported an attributable net income of $37.7 million in 2024, down from $100.8 million a year earlier.

Earnings per share were $0.04, a decline from $0.12 in the same period last year, according to a Wednesday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Revenue dropped to $630.2 million from $838.0 million the previous year.

Looking ahead, the biotech firm expects to generate a consolidated revenue of $350 million to $450 million for its oncology and immunology business.

data.title: Irish Residential Properties REIT Launches EUR5 Million Share Buyback Program
data.description:
Irish Residential Properties REIT (IRES.IR, ) on Thursday launched a program to repurchase up to 5 million euros of its shares.

The real estate investment trust will buy back the shares on Euronext Dublin by May 31 to reduce its issued capital, according to a Thursday release.

The company can repurchase up to 79,436,841 shares, or 15% of its issued capital, under the general authority approved by shareholders at its annual general meeting in May 2024.

data.title: Zytronic Names Acting CEO
data.description:
Zytronic appointed Claire Smith as its acting chief executive officer, effective Wednesday.

Smith previously served as the British touch sensor technology company's chief financial officer and will manage the company's wind-down, according to Thursday filing.

data.title: Shinko Electric Industries to See Major Shareholder Changes After Completion of Tender Offer
data.description:
Shinko Electric Industries said it saw changes in major shareholders after the completion of the tender offer by JICC-04 on March 18, according to its Wednesday bourse filing.

JICC-04 will become a major shareholder, holding at least 20% of the company's voting rights, effective March 26.

Its parent company, JIC PEFJ1 Limited Partnership, will also be classified as an affiliated company due to its indirect ownership of company shares.

The tender offeror plans to initiate procedures to make the company a wholly-owned subsidiary, which may lead to the delisting of its shares from the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

data.description: ANTA Sports Products has an average rating of buy and mean price target of HK$112.82, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: REG - ACG Metals Ltd. - Change in ISIN
data.description:
RNS Number : 4957B ACG Metals Limited 20 March 2025  

THIS ANNOUNCEMENT IS IMPORTANT AND REQUIRES THE IMMEDIATE ATTENTION OF SHAREHOLDERS. IF SHAREHOLDERS ARE IN ANY DOUBT AS TO THE ACTION THEY SHOULD TAKE, THEY SHOULD SEEK THEIR OWN INDEPENDENT PROFESSIONAL ADVISORS IMMEDIATELY.

20 March 2025

ACG METALS LIMITED

("ACG" or the "Company")

Change in ISIN

In connection with the completion of the Share Tender Offer and following the exercise by the Company of its Drag Right, the Company has today made an application to switch the Warrants ISIN. 

The Warrant's new ISIN is VGG0056A1295 (the "New ISIN") and the new SEDOL number is BTCJBR1.

The New ISIN will become effective as at 8:00 a.m. on 21 March 2025.

Following this change in ISIN, Warrantholders are required to take no further action.

Capitalised terms used but not defined in this announcement will have the same meaning as set out in the Circular published on 7 February 2025.

- ENDS -

 For further information please contact:

Palatine      

 Communications Advisor

Conal Walsh / James Gilheany/ Kelsey Traynor/ Richard Seed

 acg@palatine-media.com

About the Company

ACG is a company with a vision to consolidate the critical metals industry, starting with the copper sector. Through a series of roll-up acquisitions, ACG intends to become a premier supplier of copper and other critical metals to the western OEM supply chain, with best-in-class ESG and carbon footprint characteristics.

In September 2024, ACG successfully completed the acquisition of the Gediktepe Mine, an operating open pit mine in Türkiye currently producing 34 koz of gold and 361 koz of silver. Gediktepe is expected to transition to primary copper and zinc production from 2026 and will target annual steady-state copper equivalent production of 20-25 kt.

ACG's team has extensive M&A experience built through decades spent at blue-chip multinationals in the sector. The team brings a significant network as well as a commitment to ESG principles and strong corporate governance.

For more information about ACG, please visit: https://acgmetals.com/
